key_points: Electric current flows only when a circuit forms a complete closed path
Cells provide the electrical energy needed for the flow of current
Circuit components include cells, bulbs, switches, wires and resistive elements
Current flows from the positive terminal of a cell to the negative terminal through the circuit
Different materials act as conductors or insulators depending on their ability to allow current flow
examples: Closed circuit example: Cell → switch ON → bulb glows
Open circuit example: Switch OFF → bulb does not glow
Series connection: Two bulbs connected one after another in the same path
Testing materials: Using a simple circuit to check whether an object conducts electricity
formulas: Electric current → Flow of electric charges through a conductor
Circuit condition → Current flows only when circuit is closed
Conductor → Material that allows current to pass easily
exam_tips: Understand how current flows through a complete circuit
Learn the function of each component in a circuit
Practice identifying open and closed circuits in diagrams
Remember common examples of conductors and insulators
